results.message: Caption reading speed 240 wpm exceeds 160 wpm educational / near-verbatim cap (DCMP Captioning Key, Standard 3).
results.citation: The Described and Captioned Media Program Captioning Key states: 'Reading speed should not exceed 160 wpm for educational material and 225 wpm as an absolute maximum for adult material.'

results.message: Caption line length 44 characters exceeds 42-character Netflix limit (Netflix Timed Text Style Guide, Section 2.3).
results.citation: Netflix Timed Text Style Guide: 'Maximum 42 characters per line for English.'

results.message: Caption display duration 1.2s is below the 2-second minimum (DCMP Captioning Key, Standard 4).
results.citation: DCMP Captioning Key Standard 4: 'A caption should be displayed for a minimum of 2 seconds.'

results.message: Sound effect '[door creaks]' lacks a bracketed source identifier (DCMP Captioning Key, Standard 5).
results.citation: DCMP Captioning Key Standard 5: 'Sound effects should be identified with the source in brackets, e.g. [DOOR CREAKS].'

results.message: Caption cue at 89.4s is 2.1s ahead of its speech region onset (FCC 47 CFR 79.1(j)(2)(ii): synchronous).
results.citation: FCC 47 CFR 79.1(j)(2)(ii): Captions must be 'synchronous with the audio of the programming to the greatest extent possible.'

results.message: Caption coverage drops to 0% for a 4.2-second spoken segment at 118.0s (FCC 47 CFR 79.1(j)(2)(iii): complete).
results.citation: FCC 47 CFR 79.1(j)(2)(iii): Captions must be 'complete, meaning they include all dialogue and audible sounds.'

results.message: Two caption cues overlap at 134.7s (FCC 47 CFR 79.1(j)(2)(iv): appropriately placed).
results.citation: FCC 47 CFR 79.1(j)(2)(iv): Captions must be 'appropriately placed so as not to obscure other important visual information.'

results.message: AD line at gap 67.2s uses past tense ('walked') — DCMP requires present tense (DCMP Description Key, Standard 1).
results.citation: DCMP Description Key Standard 1: 'Use present tense when describing action on screen.'

results.message: AD line contains possible cinematic jargon ('low-angle shot') — review for objectivity (DCMP Description Key, Standard 3).
results.citation: DCMP Description Key Standard 3: 'Avoid technical film terms and jargon that might not be understood by the listener.'

results.message: AD line at 102.4s overlaps speech region by 1.8s (DCMP Description Key, Standard 5).
results.citation: DCMP Description Key Standard 5: 'Audio description must not overlap dialogue or other essential audio.'

results.message: NER caption accuracy 94.1% (band 92.1%–96.1%) is below the 98% broadcast threshold but low-confidence ASR regions present — flagged for human review, not auto-failed (Koenecke et al., PNAS 2020 ASR disparity).
results.citation: FCC 47 CFR 79.1(j)(2)(i): Captions must be 'accurate' — (total words minus errors) / total words. Band reflects ASR confidence; regions below threshold are flagged for human review per AGENTS.md responsible-AI constraint.

results.message: Captions present for all pre-recorded audio content (WCAG 2.2 SC 1.2.2, Level A).
results.citation: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.2.2: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

results.message: Audio description sidecar file present for pre-recorded video (WCAG 2.2 SC 1.2.5, Level AA).
results.citation: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.2.5: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.
